mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during known recessionary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of industrial production and new housing starts captures the aggregate demand-supply balance; when INDPRO rises while HOUST falls, it indicates supply constraints outpacing demand, leading to inflationary pressures that compress equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window, or the combined signal shows no predictive power for forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising real activity measured by CPI and GDP growth forces market expectations of tighter policy, which negatively impacts equity valuations over the next 20 trading days as discount rates adjust upward.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL and GDPC1 both rise concurrently compared to baseline periods, or the signal fails to anticipate rate-hike-driven equity corrections.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in employment growth, defined by PAYEMS falling below 100,000 new jobs for two consecutive months, signals an imminent economic slowdown that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward IC remains above 0.05 during triggered periods, or forward returns are not statistically lower than non-triggered periods, indicating the threshold lacks discriminatory power.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer credit (RSAFS) and stable short-term rates (FEDFUNDS) reflects accommodative financial conditions that fuel equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as leverage supports corporate earnings.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses sign or becomes statistically insignificant over any 2-year rolling window, or rising credit fails to translate into positive equity performance during low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets, rising industrial production fails to translate into higher corporate profits due to wage-push inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as margin compression dominates revenue growth.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth consistently predicts positive equity returns regardless of UNRATE levels, invalidating the regime dependency and suggesting labor costs do not materially impact forward valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as markets rapidly price in improved macroeconomic fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant difference between positive and negative GDP growth months, indicating decoupling between macro growth and equity pricing.
